mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month PAYEMS growth signals expanding household income and earnings breadth; higher employment momentum drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 143774 thousand as of the latest available monthly snapshot
regime_hint: post-recession expansion with positive payroll momentum
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS 3-month growth has Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window, or if top-quintile PAYEMS momentum does not outperform bottom-quintile momentum.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above its 6-month average by at least 10 basis points, discount-rate expectations tighten and equity duration is repriced lower, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its 6-month average by at least 0.10 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.36 percent in the latest available monthly snapshot after the initial post-crisis rate hike
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time, or if mean triggered returns are not below unconditional mean returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL combined with rising FEDFUNDS indicates inflation pressure forcing policy tightening; the joint condition compresses valuation multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS is above its 3-month average
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL was 237.92 and FEDFUNDS was 0.36 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS trigger has no incremental explanatory power over either single series in walk-forward tests, or if 60-day triggered mean returns exceed unconditional mean returns.

mechanism_narrative: A break of INDPRO below its 12-month moving average signals industrial contraction and weaker operating leverage, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 103.3759 in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside-crossing episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if downside crossings do not underperform upside crossings.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS year-over-year growth crosses above 4%, stronger household demand raises near-term revenue expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 446887 million dollars in the latest available monthly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS upside-growth crossings fail to generate positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered observations, or if triggered returns are not above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST with FEDFUNDS below 1% signals rate-supported construction demand and credit-sensitive cyclicality, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average rises above its 12-month average while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1178 thousand units and FEDFUNDS was 0.36 percent in the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the HOUST-FEDFUNDS interaction term has insignificant out-of-sample predictive contribution, or if triggered 60-day S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: UNRATE at or below 5% combined with strong PAYEMS growth marks late-cycle labor tightness; wage pressure and Fed reaction risk predict negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is at or below 5.0% and PAYEMS 3-month average monthly gain exceeds 150 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 5.0 percent and PAYEMS was 143774 thousand in the latest available monthly snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening labor market
failure_condition: Reject if this joint labor-tightness trigger is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or if PAYEMS adds no predictive power conditional on UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: A GDPC1 growth slowdown below 1.5% annualized over two quarters signals weak aggregate demand and declining earnings revisions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 two-quarter annualized growth crosses below 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 16470.6 billion chained 2017 dollars in the latest available quarterly snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 slowdown crossings do not precede below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the signal Rank IC is below 0.03 in walk-forward evaluation.
